programs.name: Service Payments for the Elderly and Disabled (SPED)
programs._raw:
{
  "name": "Service Payments for the Elderly and Disabled (SPED)",
  "eligibility": {
    "age": null,
    "income_limits": "Sliding scale based on countable income after deductions (child support, medical up to $500 individual/$850 family, child care, alimony, prescription drugs) and family size; exact dollar amounts not specified in sources but determines share of costs via SFN 820 form and fee schedules. No hard upper limit stated, but tied to Medicaid non-eligibility and functional needs.[1][3][5]",
    "asset_limits": "Liquid assets under $50,000 for full eligibility; $0-$24,999 uses sliding fee schedule 1, $25,000-$50,000 uses schedule 2. Counts: cash (currency, checking/savings/money market/CDs), bonds (US savings/treasury/other), retirement (IRAs, Keogh, 401(k), 403(b), annuities). Home value exempt. Deductions applied to resources.[3][5]",
    "other_requirements": [
      "Functional impairment in at least 2 ADLs or 4 IADLs, lasting or expected to last 3+ months (documented in comprehensive assessment).[1][3]",
      "Impairments not due to intellectual disability, related condition, or mental illness.[1]",
      "Living in North Dakota private family dwelling (not institution).[1]",
      "Not eligible for Medicaid (Medicaid-eligible go to Medicaid HCBS instead).[1][3]",
      "Determined via HCBS comprehensive assessment and entry into SPED Pool using SFN 1820 and SFN 676.[1]"
    ]
  },
  "benefits": {
    "type": "financial",
    "value": "Payments to providers (including family caregivers living with recipient) up to $48/day; respite up to $1,142/month; example max service amount $224 after medically needy amount ($1,174) and $20 disregard from $1,418 income; specific services via fee schedule (sliding based on income/assets); covers home/community services like family home care, homemaker, meals, etc., as alternative to basic care facility.[1][3]",
    "varies_by": "priority_tier"
  },
  "application": {
    "methods": [
      "Contact local HCBS Case Manager (no specific phone/website listed; start via ND DHS Aging Services/HCBS).[1]",
      "Submit SFN 1820 (SPED Program Pool Data) and SFN 676 (Add New Record to MMIS) to HCBS Program Administration after comprehensive assessment.[1]",
      "SFN 820 (SPED Income and Asset form) for financials.[1][5]"
    ],
    "processing_time": "Not specified; services can be authorized pending Medicaid determination if immediate need.[1]",
    "waitlist": "SPED Pool entry required before services; must be removed from pool post-eligibility notification via SFN 676.[1][2]",
    "forms": [
      "SFN 820 (Income and Assets)",
      "SFN 1820 (Program Pool Data)",
      "SFN 676 (MMIS Eligibility File)"
    ],
    "documents": [
      "Comprehensive assessment documenting impairments (3+ months duration, not ID/mental illness).[1]",
      "Proof of income/resources (e.g., statements for cash, bonds, retirement).[5]",
      "Residency verification (private family dwelling in ND).[1]"
    ]
  },
  "geography": {
    "statewide": true,
    "restrictions": "North Dakota residents only; private family dwelling (not institution).[1]",
    "regional_variations": "Administered via local HCBS Case Managers; potential regional wait times/provider differences not detailed.[1][3]",
    "offices_or_providers": ["Local HCBS Case Managers statewide; providers must enroll (family caregivers eligible if living with recipient)."][1][3]
  },
  "official_source": "https://www.nd.gov/dhs/policymanuals/52505/Content/525_05_25_20.htm",
  "gotchas": [
    "Not for Medicaid-eligible (they go to Medicaid HCBS); pending Medicaid check allowed for urgent needs.[1]",
    "Estate recovery on general fund payments for listed services (adult day care, foster care, etc.).[2]",
    "Must enter SPED Pool first; services not authorized until pool removal/approval.[1][2]",
    "Impairments exclude intellectual disability/mental illness; must be 3+ months.[1]",
    "Sliding fees reduce benefits based on income/assets over thresholds; home exempt but liquid assets capped.[3][5]"
  ],
  "data_shape_notes": "Two-tier sliding fee schedules by asset bands ($0-24,999 schedule 1, $25k-50k schedule 2); pool-based prioritization; family caregivers payable up to $48/day; state-funded non-Medicaid alternative with estate recovery."
}

programs.name: North Dakota Basic Care Assistance Program (BCAP)

eligibility.age: 65 or older OR 18 or older and blind or disabled
eligibility.income_limits: Determined by North Dakota Medicaid eligibility requirements (no specific dollar amounts or household size table provided in program sources; for context, related long-term care Medicaid has asset limit of $3,000 for singles and income mostly paid toward care minus $150 personal needs allowance)
eligibility.asset_limits: Determined by North Dakota Medicaid eligibility requirements (no specific details on what counts or exemptions in BCAP sources; Medicaid long-term care context limits assets to $3,000 for singles)
other_requirements: Functional assessment showing need for assistance with activities of daily living (ADLs) such as bathing, dressing, eating, toileting
Reside in a licensed basic care facility
For some cases: resided in nursing facility at least 6 months and meet additional criteria like subsequent denial of nursing facility level of care

benefits.value: Pays portion of costs for care in licensed basic care facility; recipient keeps $150/month personal needs allowance, other income applied to facility/health costs, BCAP covers remainder directly to facility

methods: Online: Apply for Assistance (via Customer Support Center)
Mail: Customer Support Center, P.O. Box 5562, Bismarck, ND 58506

In-person/alternative: Contact Aging and Disability Resource Link (ADRL), submit Basic Care application or Medicaid Application for the Elderly and Disabled (SFN 958) to eligibility unit, email agingbcreferrals@nd.gov
application.processing_time: Date of eligibility is date of application if qualified (no specific processing timeline stated)
forms: Medicaid Application for the Elderly and Disabled (SFN 958)
Personal Care Services Assessment (by county social worker)
documents: SOLQ may verify SSN, age, identity, social security benefits

geography.restrictions: Must reside in licensed basic care facility in North Dakota
geography.regional_variations: Administered potentially through county social workers; no explicit differences in benefits, but case management and assessments may involve local offices (no specific regional wait times or provider differences noted)

gotchas: Must first qualify for ND Medicaid (separate income/asset test, not detailed in BCAP pages)
Requires living in a licensed basic care facility (not home care or assisted living)
Income beyond $150 personal needs goes to facility/medical costs
Functional assessment required for ADLs; may not cover if only personal care assessment fails
If already on Medicaid, uses redetermination
programs.data_shape_notes: Tied directly to Medicaid eligibility (income/assets not restated in BCAP docs); requires licensed facility residence and functional ADL assessment; financial aid scales inversely with income after personal allowance
programs._raw:
{
  "name": "North Dakota Basic Care Assistance Program (BCAP)",
  "eligibility": {
    "age": "65 or older OR 18 or older and blind or disabled",
    "income_limits": "Determined by North Dakota Medicaid eligibility requirements (no specific dollar amounts or household size table provided in program sources; for context, related long-term care Medicaid has asset limit of $3,000 for singles and income mostly paid toward care minus $150 personal needs allowance)",
    "asset_limits": "Determined by North Dakota Medicaid eligibility requirements (no specific details on what counts or exemptions in BCAP sources; Medicaid long-term care context limits assets to $3,000 for singles)",
    "other_requirements": [
      "North Dakota resident",
      "Qualify for North Dakota Medicaid",
      "Functional assessment showing need for assistance with activities of daily living (ADLs) such as bathing, dressing, eating, toileting",
      "Reside in a licensed basic care facility",
      "For some cases: resided in nursing facility at least 6 months and meet additional criteria like subsequent denial of nursing facility level of care"
    ]
  },
  "benefits": {
    "type": "financial",
    "value": "Pays portion of costs for care in licensed basic care facility; recipient keeps $150/month personal needs allowance, other income applied to facility/health costs, BCAP covers remainder directly to facility",
    "varies_by": "income"
  },
  "application": {
    "methods": [
      "Online: Apply for Assistance (via Customer Support Center)",
      "Phone: (866) 614-6005",
      "Email: applyforhelp@nd.gov",
      "Fax: (701) 328-1006",
      "Mail: Customer Support Center, P.O. Box 5562, Bismarck, ND 58506",
      "In-person/alternative: Contact Aging and Disability Resource Link (ADRL), submit Basic Care application or Medicaid Application for the Elderly and Disabled (SFN 958) to eligibility unit, email agingbcreferrals@nd.gov"
    ],
    "processing_time": "Date of eligibility is date of application if qualified (no specific processing timeline stated)",
    "waitlist": null,
    "forms": [
      "Application for Assistance",
      "Medicaid Application for the Elderly and Disabled (SFN 958)",
      "Personal Care Services Assessment (by county social worker)",
      "Transmittal Between Units (SFN 21)"
    ],
    "documents": [
      "Social security number",
      "Proof of age",
      "Proof of residence",
      "Proof of disability (if applicable)",
      "Financial information for Medicaid eligibility",
      "SOLQ may verify SSN, age, identity, social security benefits"
    ]
  },
  "geography": {
    "statewide": true,
    "restrictions": "Must reside in licensed basic care facility in North Dakota",
    "regional_variations": "Administered potentially through county social workers; no explicit differences in benefits, but case management and assessments may involve local offices (no specific regional wait times or provider differences noted)",
    "offices_or_providers": [
      "Customer Support Center (statewide)",
      "County social workers for assessments",
      "Licensed basic care facilities statewide"
    ]
  },
  "official_source": "https://www.hhs.nd.gov/adults-and-aging/services/basic-care-assistance-program-bcap",
  "gotchas": [
    "Must first qualify for ND Medicaid (separate income/asset test, not detailed in BCAP pages)",
    "Requires living in a licensed basic care facility (not home care or assisted living)",
    "Income beyond $150 personal needs goes to facility/medical costs",
    "Functional assessment required for ADLs; may not cover if only personal care assessment fails",
    "If already on Medicaid, uses redetermination"
  ],
  "data_shape_notes": "Tied directly to Medicaid eligibility (income/assets not restated in BCAP docs); requires licensed facility residence and functional ADL assessment; financial aid scales inversely with income after personal allowance"
}

programs.name: North Dakota Aging in Community (AIC) Project

eligibility.income_limits: No specific income limits stated; focuses on older adults in rural areas without financial thresholds detailed in available sources.
eligibility.asset_limits: No asset limits specified for AIC.
other_requirements: Older adult (typically 60+ implied) living in targeted rural communities
Desire to age in place in rural home
Residency in implementation areas such as Lisbon and western Morton Counties

benefits.value: Community-based supports including volunteer assistance for transportation, food and nutrition classes, fall prevention education, technology assistance; fosters connections to local resources to enhance quality of life, safety, and independence.

methods: Contact NDSU Extension (specific phone/website not listed; start via NDSU Agriculture Extension at ndsu.edu/agriculture/extension)
Community-based model; engage local AIC project coordinators in targeted rural areas
documents: No specific documents listed; needs assessment may be involved via local coordinators

geography.restrictions: Limited to specific rural areas, e.g., Lisbon and western Morton Counties; based on community-based models
geography.regional_variations: Implemented in select rural communities with tailored resources; different local providers and supports by location (e.g., NDSU Extension in targeted counties)
offices_or_providers: NDSU Extension Service (Lisbon, western Morton Counties)

gotchas: Not a statewide entitlement program—limited to specific rural pilot areas, not available everywhere in ND
Not a direct financial aid or formal healthcare service like Medicaid/SPED; relies on volunteer and community connections
People may confuse with state programs like SPED (which has income/asset limits and payments) or Medicaid waivers
Focuses on prevention and connections rather than intensive medical care
Success measured in connections and confidence, not guaranteed services or hours
programs.data_shape_notes: Community-based pilot in select rural counties only; no formal income/asset tests or fixed benefits; volunteer-driven model to fill service gaps, not a traditional entitlement program

programs.name: ND Assistive Senior Safety Program

eligibility.income_limits: Income guidelines apply (specific dollar amounts and household size variations detailed in the application form; e.g., 2024 and 2025 application PDFs reference guidelines but do not list exact figures in available excerpts)
other_requirements: Need for assistive safety devices to remain safely in home
Does not qualify for devices through insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, or other programs (similar to related Possibilities Grant criteria)

benefits.value: Free assistive safety devices including: Alerting Devices for Hearing Loss, Anti-Elopement Devices, and other home safety equipment; professional setup assistance provided

methods: Mail: ND Assistive/ Senior Safety, 3240 15th St. S, Suite B, Fargo, ND 58104
application.processing_time: Typically 3-4 weeks (based on similar program review process)
forms: Request For North Dakota Assistive Safety Devices Distribution (Senior Safety Application)
documents: Completed application listing requested devices in order of importance
Proof of income to meet guidelines
Proof of non-qualification from other funding sources if required

offices_or_providers: Central office: 3240 15th St. S, Suite B, Fargo, ND 58104

gotchas: Must exhaust other funding sources first (insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, etc.) — program is last resort
List devices in order of importance on application; only one per line
Income guidelines in application — review current year form (e.g., 2025) as they may update annually
Available to those in assisted living if applicable
programs.data_shape_notes: Statewide but administered from Fargo office; prioritizes home safety for aging in place; income-tested with guidelines in forms; device requests ranked by applicant
programs._raw:
